findings.issue: Root Dockerfile pnpm version hardcoded; CI assertion gap lets it drift from package.json
findings.citation: Dockerfile:31 — `RUN npm install -g pnpm@10.25.0 && pnpm install` (hardcoded literal). crates/remote/Dockerfile:8-11 — uses `ARG PNPM_VERSION` + `corepack prepare "pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION}" --activate` (driven by compose build-arg). The CI step at .github/workflows/remote-hive-build.yml:44 only verifies the docker-compose.yml fallback against package.json; it never checks the root Dockerfile literal. Bumping packageManager in package.json will not be caught for the root Dockerfile.
findings.remediation:
Add `ARG PNPM_VERSION=10.25.0` to the root Dockerfile and replace the hardcoded install line:

ARG PNPM_VERSION=10.25.0
RUN npm install -g pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION} && pnpm install

Alternatively, extend the CI assertion in remote-hive-build.yml to also grep the root Dockerfile:

ROOT_PNPM=$(grep -oE 'pnpm@[0-9.]+' Dockerfile | head -1 | sed 's/pnpm@//')
[ "$ROOT_PNPM" = "$EXPECTED_VERSION" ] || { echo "Root Dockerfile pnpm@$ROOT_PNPM != package.json $EXPECTED_VERSION"; exit 1; }

findings.issue: `ARG PNPM_VERSION` in crates/remote/Dockerfile has no default; bare `docker build` produces invalid corepack invocation
findings.citation: crates/remote/Dockerfile:9 — `ARG PNPM_VERSION` (no default value declared). crates/remote/Dockerfile:10-11 — `RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare "pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION}" --activate`. When PNPM_VERSION is unset, this expands to `corepack prepare "pnpm@"` which fails. The docker-compose.yml fallback (line 123: `PNPM_VERSION: ${PNPM_VERSION:-10.25.0}`) only protects the docker-compose path; a direct `docker build -f crates/remote/Dockerfile .` has no such protection.

.github/workflows/remote-hive-build.yml:78 — diff annotation `\ No newline at end of file` on the final `echo "BUILD PASSED: no ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE"` line. Functionally harmless but violates POSIX text-file convention.
findings.remediation: Add a trailing newline after line 78.

findings.issue: Unexplained `debug` transitive dependency downgrade from 4.4.3 to 4.4.1 in pnpm lockfile snapshots
findings.citation: pnpm-lock.yaml — http-proxy-agent and https-proxy-agent snapshots: `-      debug: 4.4.3` / `+      debug: 4.4.1`. No entry in the diff explains why the resolved version regressed. This indicates lock file regeneration touched more than the intended i18next/react-i18next additions.
findings.remediation: Run `pnpm install` from a clean state and verify the lockfile stabilises at 4.4.3 (or the latest resolved version). If 4.4.1 is the correct resolution, add a comment explaining the constraint that forces it.

findings.issue: engines.node CI threshold hardcoded to 22, decoupled from actual package.json constraint
findings.citation: .github/workflows/remote-hive-build.yml:56 — `[ "$MAJOR" -ge 22 ]`. package.json engines.node is now `>=22.13`. If a future update raises engines.node to `>=24`, this check still passes with any node >=22 engine string, masking a more permissive-than-intended constraint.
findings.remediation: Derive the threshold dynamically from the package.json engines.node value itself rather than hardcoding 22, or document the intentional floor explicitly.
